hub_subtitle: Inglês para a vida real — falar, entender e ter autonomia no idioma

lesson.menu_title: Growing Up in Butantan -- From São Paulo to the Countryside
lesson.menu_desc: Telling the story of where you come from and why you left -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 2 -- Growing Up in Butantan
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Ana Claudia Veraldi | Lesson 2 -- Growing Up in Butantan

listenings.text: I grew up in a small apartment above my grandmother's bakery. Back then the street was full of children, and we played outside until it got dark. My mother was always working, so my grandmother raised me, really. I remember the smell of bread every single morning. The neighborhood is completely different now. The bakery closed in two thousand and four, and there is a bank there today. Last year I walked past the building while I was visiting the city, and honestly, I did not recognize anything except the door.

I remember the exact day I decided to leave. It was a Tuesday, and it was raining. I was sitting in a traffic jam downtown, and I was not even angry anymore. I was just tired. The radio was playing something I did not like, and I looked at the clock and I realized I had been in that car for one hour and forty minutes. And I thought, this is my life. I am spending my life here, in this car. Two months later we sold the apartment. People said I was crazy. I was not crazy. I was just done.

extra_audio.text: First, Tom asks Ana where she grew up. Then Ana says she spent her childhood in Butantan, in Sao Paulo. Next, she describes what the neighborhood was like back then. After that, she tells him about the day she was sitting in traffic and decided to leave. Finally, Tom says he did something very similar, and they laugh about it.
